routines.name: search_vehicles_by_make
routines.description: Look up all vehicles matching a given make, with availability status and location details
routines.instructions: SELECT vehicle_id, make, model, year, category, daily_rate, is_available, license_plate, l.name as location_name, l.city, l.state FROM vehicles v LEFT JOIN locations l ON v.location_id = l.location_id WHERE LOWER(v.make) = LOWER('{make}') ORDER BY year DESC, model ASC. Return all columns listed. Use case-insensitive matching on make.

routines.name: search_available_vehicles_by_make
routines.description: Look up only currently available vehicles for a given make, optionally filtered by city
routines.instructions: SELECT vehicle_id, make, model, year, category, daily_rate, license_plate, l.name as location_name, l.city, l.state, l.airport_code FROM vehicles v JOIN locations l ON v.location_id = l.location_id WHERE LOWER(v.make) = LOWER('{make}') AND v.is_available = TRUE AND ('{city}' = '' OR LOWER(l.city) = LOWER('{city}')) ORDER BY daily_rate ASC. Filter to is_available = TRUE. If city param is empty string, skip city filter.

routines.name: get_vehicle_makes
routines.description: Return a distinct list of all vehicle makes in the fleet along with a count of total and available vehicles per make
routines.instructions: SELECT make, COUNT(*) as total_vehicles, SUM(CASE WHEN is_available THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as available_vehicles FROM vehicles GROUP BY make ORDER BY make ASC. No parameters needed. Useful for helping users discover what makes are offered.

routines.name: search_vehicles_by_make_and_category
routines.description: Look up vehicles filtered by make and vehicle category (e.g. SUV, Sedan, Truck)
routines.instructions: SELECT vehicle_id, make, model, year, category, daily_rate, is_available, license_plate, l.name as location_name, l.city, l.state FROM vehicles v LEFT JOIN locations l ON v.location_id = l.location_id WHERE LOWER(v.make) = LOWER('{make}') AND ('{category}' = '' OR LOWER(v.category) = LOWER('{category}')) ORDER BY is_available DESC, daily_rate ASC. If category is empty string, return all categories for that make. Sort available vehicles first.

routines.name: get_vehicle_details
routines.description: Retrieve full details for a specific vehicle by vehicle_id, including location and active reservation info
routines.instructions: SELECT v.*, l.name as location_name, l.city, l.state, l.airport_code, l.phone as location_phone, r.reservation_id as active_reservation_id, r.pickup_date, r.dropoff_date FROM vehicles v LEFT JOIN locations l ON v.location_id = l.location_id LEFT JOIN reservations r ON v.vehicle_id = r.vehicle_id AND r.status IN ('confirmed','active') AND r.dropoff_date >= CURRENT_DATE WHERE v.vehicle_id = '{vehicle_id}'. Return one row with full vehicle, location, and any active reservation details.

routines.name: update_vehicle_availability
routines.description: Update the availability status of a vehicle by vehicle_id
routines.instructions: CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE with IN parameters vehicle_id STRING and is_available BOOLEAN. Execute UPDATE on vehicles table setting is_available = is_available WHERE vehicle_id = vehicle_id parameter. Raise an error using SIGNAL if no rows are affected (vehicle not found). Include a SELECT to confirm the updated row after the update.

routines.name: update_vehicle_daily_rate
routines.description: Update the daily rental rate for a specific vehicle
routines.instructions: CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE with IN parameters vehicle_id STRING and new_daily_rate DOUBLE. Validate that new_daily_rate > 0, otherwise SIGNAL an error with message 'Daily rate must be greater than zero'. Execute UPDATE on vehicles table setting daily_rate = new_daily_rate WHERE vehicle_id matches. If no rows updated, SIGNAL an error that the vehicle was not found.

routines.name: transfer_vehicle_location
routines.description: Transfer a vehicle to a new location by updating its location_id
routines.instructions: CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE with IN parameters vehicle_id STRING and new_location_id STRING. First verify the new_location_id exists in the locations table; if not, SIGNAL an error 'Location not found'. Then UPDATE vehicles SET location_id = new_location_id WHERE vehicle_id matches. If no vehicle rows updated, SIGNAL 'Vehicle not found'. On success, SELECT the updated vehicle row joined with the new location details to confirm the transfer.
